description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a cooked lobster, featuring a robust reddish-orange exoskeleton with a segmented body. The cephalothorax is broad and armored, leading to a tapered, segmented abdomen that ends in a fan-shaped tail. Two large, asymmetrical claws extend from the front, one noticeably larger than the other, and are accompanied by several smaller walking legs along the sides. Long, thin antennae project forward from the head, and small eyes are positioned on short stalks. The surface appears hard and slightly glossy, with subtle textural details mimicking the natural shell of a lobster.
description: A realistic, reddish-orange cooked lobster with large claws, segmented body, and long antennae. The shell is hard and slightly glossy, with detailed anatomical features.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large claws, long antennae, and the broad cephalothorax.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the segmentation of the body, the curvature of the tail, and the profile of the claws and legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the fan-shaped tail and the symmetrical segmentation of the abdomen.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the body segments, claws, and walking legs from the opposite profile.
